title: 2026 Dallas Cowboys Schedule: Dates and Times Released
body:
The 2026 National Football League season is just around the corner, and for the Dallas Cowboys, it's a chance to put the disappointments of the previous year behind them. As the team prepares to take on a new slate of challenges, fans are eagerly anticipating the upcoming campaign, which promises to be filled with exciting matchups and thrilling gridiron action. With a renewed sense of determination and a fresh perspective, the Cowboys are looking to make a strong statement in the highly competitive NFL landscape. The release of the team's regular-season schedule has only added to the excitement, giving fans a glimpse into the exciting journey that lies ahead.

The Cowboys' 2025 season was marked by disappointment and unfulfilled expectations, but the team is determined to learn from its mistakes and use them as a stepping stone for improvement. With a strong roster and a talented squad, the Cowboys have the potential to make a deep playoff run and bring home a championship. Quarterback Dak Prescott, who has been a stalwart leader for the team, will once again be at the helm, guiding his teammates through the ups and downs of the season. The addition of new players and the development of existing ones will also be crucial in determining the team's success, as they look to build a cohesive unit that can compete with the best teams in the league.

As the Cowboys embark on their 2026 journey, they will face a tough schedule that includes matchups against some of the NFL's top teams. The team's ability to navigate these challenges will be a key factor in determining their overall success, and fans will be watching with bated breath as they take on their divisional rivals and other top contenders. The Cowboys' home games at AT&T Stadium are always highly anticipated events, and this season promises to be no exception, with a electric atmosphere and a sea of loyal fans cheering on their team. With the schedule now officially released, fans can start making plans to attend games and show their support for the team.

The NFL is a highly competitive league, and the Cowboys will need to be at the top of their game if they hope to achieve their goals. The team's coaching staff, led by their experienced head coach, will play a crucial role in preparing the players for the challenges ahead and making strategic decisions that can make all the difference between victory and defeat. The Cowboys' front office has also been busy during the offseason, making key moves to strengthen the roster and address areas of concern. As the team looks to turn the page on a disappointing 2025 season, they will be relying on a combination of veteran leadership, young talent, and savvy coaching to guide them through the ups and downs of the season.

The release of the Cowboys' schedule has also sparked excitement among fans, who are eagerly anticipating the opportunity to see their team in action. The NFL has a massive following, and the Cowboys are one of the most beloved and recognizable teams in the league. With a huge national and international fan base, the team's games are always highly anticipated events, and this season promises to be no exception. As the Cowboys prepare to take on the best teams in the league, their fans will be cheering them on every step of the way, providing a vital source of support and motivation that can make all the difference in the world.

As the 2026 NFL season approaches, the Dallas Cowboys are ready to put the past behind them and focus on the exciting journey ahead. With a tough schedule, a talented roster, and a loyal fan base, the team has all the ingredients necessary to make a deep playoff run and bring home a championship. As the team takes to the field for the first game of the season, fans will be watching with bated breath, eager to see what the future holds for their beloved Cowboys. One thing is certain, however: the 2026 NFL season promises to be an exciting and unpredictable ride, full of twists and turns that will keep fans on the edge of their seats until the very end.
